§6Uranium Hexafluoride§f

§aThe gaseous intermediary for uranium enrichment — turn yellowcake into enriched fuel!§f

§bUranium Hexafluoride§f (UF6) is a corrosive radioactive gas and the final step before enrichment. It must be processed in a §bGas Centrifuge§f to separate U-235 from U-238.

§6Production§f (Chemical Plant, 5s, 500HE/t):
§b• §e1,000mB§f Water
§b• §bYellowcake§f
§b• §e4×§f Fluorite
→ §e1,200mB§f §bUF6§f + §e2×§f Sulfur

§6Storage§f:
§b• Requires§f a §bspecialized tank§f
§b• Stored in§f Fluid Barrels (empty and filled variants)

§6Processing§f:
§b• Gas Centrifuge§f: processes UF6 to separate U-238 (majority) from U-235 (small amount)
§b• SILEX§f: can also process UF6 for faster enrichment

§6Other Uses§f:
§b• Pulsating Crystal§f: crafted using UF6 in cells

§c⚠ Safety§f: UF6 is both §bcorrosive§f and §bradioactive§f. Handle with proper PPE!

§eTip§f: One batch of §e1,200mB§f UF6 feeds a full Gas Centrifuge cascade. Scale up Yellowcake production before starting enrichment — you'll need a lot!
